info.name: Axxon One Integration APIs
info.description:
HTTP and gRPC-over-HTTP endpoints for the Axxon One / Axxon Next Integration APIs.

## Setup
1. Open the collection **Variables** tab and set `baseUrl`, `username`, `password`, and `hostname` to match your server.
2. Basic Auth is configured at the collection level, so every request inherits it.
3. To use **Bearer** auth on endpoints that refuse Basic Auth (e.g. some `/archive/media/...` calls that return `401 missing auth token` with `grpcErrorCode: 16`):
   - Run **Auth / Get token (AuthenticateEx)** — the test script saves the JWT into the `authToken` variable.
   - On the request that needs it, open the *Authorization* tab, select **Bearer Token**, and enter `{{authToken}}`.

## Time format
Timestamps use `YYYYMMDDTHHMMSS` in UTC+0 (e.g. `20260423T184629`). Use the literals `past` and `future` for open-ended ranges.

## VideoSourceId format
Three-part ID: `HOSTNAME/DeviceIpint.N/SourceEndpoint.video:0:0`.

variable.description: Client HTTP API host:port (separate service on port 8888).
UUID returned by /uuid, used to track archive stream sessions.

item.name: 01 · Auth & Session

request.description:
Exchange Basic-Auth credentials for a Bearer JWT.

The test script saves `token_value` into the `authToken` collection variable. Use `Authorization: Bearer {{authToken}}` on endpoints that reject Basic Auth (e.g. some `/archive/media/...` calls).

exec: // Capture the auth token from the AuthenticateEx response and
// save it to the `authToken` collection variable so other requests
// can reference it via {{authToken}} when using Bearer auth.
console.warn('No token_value in response — check credentials and body.');
console.error('Failed to parse AuthenticateEx response: ' + e.message);

request.description: Renew an active token. The token must still be valid when called.
Generates a unique identifier used for tagging archive streams.

item.description: Token acquisition + session management. Run *Get token* first if endpoints return `401 missing auth token`.

request.description: Replace `{{hostname}}` with the target server name.

Returns signed RTSP/HTTP paths for live playback.

Add `enable_token_auth=1&valid_token_hours=1` to get a self-authenticating URL playable by video players that don't support auth headers.
Replace the GUID in the URL with your group ID.

item.name: List telemetry devices on a camera

request.description: Returns the dates on which recordings exist in the given window. `beginTime`/`endTime` follow `YYYYMMDDTHHMMSS` (UTC+0).

Returns signed stream URLs for playback.

If you get `401 missing auth token`, this endpoint needs Bearer auth — run *Auth / Get token*, then on this request set Authorization to `Bearer Token` with value `{{authToken}}`.

Alternatively, append `&enable_token_auth=1&valid_token_hours=1` for self-authenticating URLs.
Works for auto/face/vmda — replace path segment accordingly.
Formats: mp4, mkv, avi, exe, jpg, pdf. See section 4.2.6.1 for the full parameter list.

item.name: 12 · gRPC over HTTP (POST /grpc)
item.description: Examples of gRPC methods proxied through the web server. The request body is always `{ "method": "<fully.qualified.Service.Method>", "data": { ... } }`.

item.name: 13 · Client HTTP API (port 8888)
item.description: Controls the desktop Client UI (layouts, displays, camera placement). Runs on a separate port from the Server API.
